channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Musk appears at White House defending DOGE's work but acknowledging mistakes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's most powerful adviser, Elon Musk, made a rare public appearance at the White House on Tuesday to defend the swift and extensive cuts he's pushing across the federal government while acknowledging there have been mistakes and will be more.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Court sets March 19 date for first execution in Arizona in over 2 years
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Arizona Supreme Court set a March 19 execution date Tuesday for a man who pleaded guilty to murder more than 17 years ago and recently said his death sentence was “long overdue.” It would be the state’s first application of the death penalty in more than two years.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daily average of known gotaways at southern border plummets, down 93% from Biden administration highs
item.description: The average daily number of known gotaways at the southern border has dropped 93% since President Donald Trump returned to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Disney, amid changes to DEI initiatives, will alter content warning ahead of 'Peter Pan,' 'Dumbo' and other older films
item.description: Amid the changes, Disney is also replacing the language in the content advisory disclaimers that auto-play before certain older films streaming on Disney+.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs rattle small business owners already dealing with tight margins
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's continued roll out of a wide array of tariffs is rattling small business owners already dealing with tight profit margins. Trump on Monday announced a 25% tariff on steel and aluminum and promised more import duties to come.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-49er Dana Stubblefield freed from prison after rape conviction overturned
item.description: A judge has granted the release of former San Francisco 49er Dana Stubblefield after his 2020 rape conviction was vacated by a California appeals court last December.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A pilot on a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer killed in Arizona aircraft collision
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One of two pilots on a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil died after the aircraft veered off a runway in Arizona and hit a business jet, according to a Federal Aviation Administration incident report.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plane that crashed and killed 10 was part of Alaska’s air travel system, essential to daily life
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The commuter flight that crashed on Alaska sea ice last week, killing all 10 aboard, was part of the workhorse network of short-hop flights that people in the immense and rugged state rely on to get to medical appointments, attend work meetings, buy essential supplies or eve
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York mayor vows to regain public's trust after Justice Department orders halt to prosecution
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams vowed to regain the public’s trust Tuesday as the Justice Department moved to halt his criminal corruption case, an extraordinary directive that officials said would free him up to assist in the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Detroit police say 2 kids died in a cold car after months without a permanent home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two children who likely froze to death in a van outside a Detroit casino had been living in the vehicle with their family for a few months, moving around when possible, authorities said Tuesday. Their mother had contacted City Hall on Nov.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 10th person charged in 'violent' Aurora apartment kidnapping
item.description: A male and female victim were allegedly tied up, pistol-whipped, beaten and stabbed by about 12 armed individuals, police said.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin's top education official facing challengers on both flanks
item.description: Wisconsin’s top education official faces challengers on the right and left as she runs for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thousands of tickets issued in Rhode Island over dark window tint
item.description: Massachusetts and Connecticut allow vehicle window tints to be significantly darker than Rhode Island does.
item.dc:creator: Tim White, NewsNation

item.title: Punk rock pioneer battling stage 4 cancer and brain tumor; family asks for help
item.description: His family is holding a fundraiser to help pay for a nurse, physical therapy, and "day-to-day vital living expenses.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Video shows speeding jet owned by Mötley Crüe's Vince Neil plow into parked plane
item.description: Dramatic video has been released showing the moment a private jet owned by Vince Neil – the lead singer of Mötley Crüe – veer off a runway in Scottsdale Airport in Arizona and smash into a parked plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Camouflaged camera found pointed at Colorado home
item.description: "I know if I was that person with a camera pointed at my house I'd be very, very concerned," Wilson said.
item.dc:creator: Kim Posey, NewsNation

item.title: How photos lost in American disasters find their way home, with a little help from people who care
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hollowed-out homes. Cars entombed by mud. Unpeopled roads. Belongings reduced to dirt and debris. It all took a toll on Taylor Schenker. After Hurricane Helene last September, Schenker was upset by the deluge of images of Asheville, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: ANNA FURMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crush of Valentine's Day flowers: Nearly a billion go through this city to you
item.description: If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: PBS closes DEI office to comply with executive order
item.description: PBS' move comes as Trump's FCC also continues to turn up the heat on public broadcasters over taxpayer funding and editorial direction.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Karen Read can be retried in Boston officer John O’Keefe's death, court rules
item.description: The ruling from the Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court clears the way for a new trial on all three charges.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Murphy looks to be guiding force for Democrats in new Trump era
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) is taking a front-and-center role in the efforts to push Democrats toward becoming the party of economic populism and to challenge President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says who could potentially fund the rebuild of war-torn enclave and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China expands influence near wealthy Florida enclave as migrants from communist country flood into US
item.description: Gordon G. Chang discussed China's growing influence in the Caribbean region and the national security threat it could present.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's education chief comes from the boardroom, not the classroom. Backers say that's her strength
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon once described herself as an outsider stepping into the world of education, a business leader with an “inquiring mind” who would challenge the status quo and demand more for students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What the Westminster show dogs' names mean
item.description: They have names that could make a random password generator cry uncle. Meet, for example, GCHG CH Calicops Sassafras Gonnakikurass. “She’s a saucy girl. Her name says it all,” Fred Ortiz said as he groomed the Brussels griffon to compete Monday at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hegseth says Fort Bragg is coming back, but with a twist
item.description: On Monday, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth signed a memo that orders the Army to rename Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, this time in honor of a different soldier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: BERKELEY, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives in programs receiving federal money has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, TERRY CHEA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4
